text.content: "You've practiced building expressions from equal groups pictures. You learned different ways to build them: choosing from options and using tiles. Now let's connect what you discovered."

text.content: 1.1  Representation Transfer Same Expression Different  [s1_1_representation_transfer_same_expression_different]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Representation Transfer Same Expression Different  [s1_1_representation_transfer_same_expression_different]

text.content: "These three pictures look different. Bags, boxes, circles. But they all show the same thing."

rich_text.plain_text: "These three pictures look different. Bags, boxes, circles. But they all show the same thing."

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["2 groups with 5 in each", "The containers (bags/boxes/circles)", "The items inside", "The colors or shapes"]
"What stays the same across all three?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected 2 groups with 5 in each, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected 2 groups with 5 in each, correct

text.content: "Right. The containers change. bags, boxes, circles. but the structure doesn't. 2 groups of 5 every time. That's what the expression 2 times 5 captures. the structure underneath."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. The containers change. bags, boxes, circles. but the structure doesn't. 2 groups of 5 every time. That's what the expression 2 times 5 captures. the structure underneath."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 'The containers (bags/boxes/circles)': focused on what changes rather than what stays the same

text.content: "The containers do change. bags, boxes, circles. But look at the expression below. What does 2 × 5 tell us about the groups?"

rich_text.plain_text: "The containers do change. bags, boxes, circles. But look at the expression below. What does 2 × 5 tell us about the groups?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 'The items inside': focused on visual details rather than mathematical structure

text.content: "The items might look different. But count the groups in each picture. How many groups? How many in each group? That's what matters."

rich_text.plain_text: "The items might look different. But count the groups in each picture. How many groups? How many in each group? That's what matters."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 'The colors or shapes': focused on surface features rather than mathematical structure

text.content: "Colors and shapes can change, but the multiplication stays the same. Look at 2 × 5. Count the groups and what's in each group across all three pictures."

rich_text.plain_text: "Colors and shapes can change, but the multiplication stays the same. Look at 2 × 5. Count the groups and what's in each group across all three pictures."

text.content: "Let me show you. In the bags: 2 groups, 5 in each. In the boxes: 2 groups, 5 in each. In the circles: 2 groups, 5 in each. The containers change, but the structure stays the same. 2 groups with 5 in each. That's what 2 × 5 means."

text.content: Four real-world scenario illustrations appear. A: Egg carton (2 rows, 6 eggs each). B: Parking lot (3 rows, 4 cars each). C: Bookshelf (5 shelves, 3 books each). D: Park benches (4 benches, varying people).

text.content: "Equal groups show up everywhere, not just in math problems. Which of these shows equal groups?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Equal groups show up everywhere, not just in math problems. Which of these shows equal groups?"

tool: multiple_choice  options: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
"Which one shows equal groups?"

tool: multiple_choice  options: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
"Which one shows equal groups?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. The egg carton. 2 rows of 6 eggs. That's 2 times 6. Equal groups in the grocery store."

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at those benches. Some have 2 people, some have 3, some have 1. Equal groups need the same number in each. Which scenarios have the same number?"

text.content: "Let me show you. The egg carton has 2 rows of 6 eggs. The parking lot has 3 rows of 4 cars. The bookshelf has 5 shelves with 3 books each. All three show equal groups because each group has the same number. D doesn't work because the benches have different numbers of people."

text.content: "Today you built expressions from pictures, and you matched pictures to expressions. Which direction made more sense to you?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Today you built expressions from pictures, and you matched pictures to expressions. Which direction made more sense to you?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Building from pictures (Visual → Expression)", "Matching pictures to expressions (Expression → Visual)", "Both helped in different ways", "I'm still figuring it out"]
"Which helped you understand expressions better?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Building from pictures (Visual → Expression)", "Matching pictures to expressions (Expression → Visual)", "Both helped in different ways", "I'm still figuring it out"]
"Which helped you understand expressions better?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected building from pictures

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected building from pictures

text.content: "Nice. Building from pictures lets you see where the numbers come from. That's a strong way to learn."

rich_text.plain_text: "Nice. Building from pictures lets you see where the numbers come from. That's a strong way to learn."

text.content: ✅ Student selected matching pictures to expressions

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected matching pictures to expressions

text.content: "Good. Working backwards, from expression to picture, shows you what expressions MEAN. That's powerful too."

rich_text.plain_text: "Good. Working backwards, from expression to picture, shows you what expressions MEAN. That's powerful too."

text.content: ✅ Student selected both helped in different ways

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected both helped in different ways

text.content: "I see. Different directions for different moments. That's flexible thinking."

rich_text.plain_text: "I see. Different directions for different moments. That's flexible thinking."

text.content: ✅ Student selected still figuring it out

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected still figuring it out

text.content: "That's okay. Still figuring it out is totally fine. You'll notice what works as you keep practicing."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's okay. Still figuring it out is totally fine. You'll notice what works as you keep practicing."

text.content: 1.4  Identity Building Closure  [s1_4_identity_building_closure]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.4  Identity Building Closure  [s1_4_identity_building_closure]

text.content: "You built expressions from equal groups pictures. You learned that 4 times 3 means 4 groups with 3 in each. You know what the numbers represent: groups and items."

rich_text.plain_text: "You built expressions from equal groups pictures. You learned that 4 times 3 means 4 groups with 3 in each. You know what the numbers represent: groups and items."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "Next time, you'll find out what 4 times 3 equals. You'll learn to figure out the total. You're ready because you already know what the expression means."

rich_text.plain_text: "Next time, you'll find out what 4 times 3 equals. You'll learn to figure out the total. You're ready because you already know what the expression means."
